---
title: "Passage Cross-References Demo"
author: "Jiangyang Zhang"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    # Use `quarto render demo.qmd` to generate demo.html
    # Add `embed-resources: true` above for a self-contained HTML file
filters:
  - passage-xref
---

## Inline Passage Targets

Wrap any inline text with `.passage` and an ID starting with `pas-`:

```markdown
[the square of the hypotenuse...]{.passage #pas-pythagoras}
```

This chapter demonstrates all theorem-like types: [theorems, lemmas, corollaries, propositions, conjectures]{.passage #pas-theorem-types}.

The fundamental result states that [the square of the hypotenuse of a right triangle equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides]{.passage #pas-pythagoras}.

In number theory, [every even integer greater than 2 can be expressed as the sum of two primes]{.passage #pas-goldbach}, a claim that remains unproven since 1742.

[The derivative of a function at a point is the limit of the difference quotient as the increment approaches zero]{.passage #pas-derivative}.

## Cross-References

Use `.pref` with the `pas` attribute to reference a passage:

```markdown
See [Passage]{.pref pas="pas-pythagoras"}.
```

- [Passage]{.pref pas="pas-theorem-types"} lists the main theorem-like types.
- [Passage]{.pref pas="pas-pythagoras"} is the classical result.
- [Passage]{.pref pas="pas-goldbach"} is a famous unsolved conjecture.
- [Passage]{.pref pas="pas-derivative"} captures the intuition behind calculus.

## Reference Options

| Option | Syntax | Output |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Default prefix | `[Passage]{.pref pas="pas-xxx"}` | "Passage N" |
| Custom prefix | `[Passage]{.pref pas="pas-xxx" prefix="Stmt"}` | "Stmt N" |
| No prefix | `[Passage]{.pref pas="pas-xxx" noprefix="true"}` | "N" |

Examples targeting the Pythagorean theorem:

- Default: [Passage]{.pref pas="pas-pythagoras"}
- Custom prefix: [Passage]{.pref pas="pas-pythagoras" prefix="Stmt"}
- No prefix: [Passage]{.pref pas="pas-pythagoras" noprefix="true"}

## Forward References

References can appear before their targets. The filter uses a two-pass design:

[Passage]{.pref pas="pas-forward-demo"} is defined below.

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.

[This passage was referenced before it was defined]{.passage #pas-forward-demo}.

## Broken References

Unresolved references display a warning:

[Passage]{.pref pas="pas-nonexistent"} should show an error indicator.
